nodes.x: Lord, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations.
Thy faithfulness is unto all generations: thou hast established the earth, and it abideth.
But thou, O Lord, shalt endure for ever; and thy remembrance unto all generations.
I have surely built thee an house to dwell in, a settled place for thee to abide in for ever.
Whereas thou hast been forsaken and hated, so that no man went through thee, I will make thee an eternal excellency, a joy of many generations.
Therefore now, Lord, let the thing that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and concerning his house be established for ever, and do as thou hast said.
Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever thou hadst formed the earth and the world, even from everlasting to everlasting, thou art God.
The sunbeams of a cloudless summer shine upon thy head ; and thy foundations are iirmly laid among' the everlasting mountains. *
The mountains melted from before the Lord, even that Sinai from before the Lord God of Israel.
When thou didst terrible things which we looked not for, thou camest down, the mountains flowed down at thy presence.
He stood, and measured the earth: he beheld, and drove asunder the nations; and the everlasting mountains were scattered, the perpetual hills did bow: his ways are everlasting.
Oh that thou wouldest rend the heavens, that thou wouldest come down, that the mountains might flow down at thy presence,
Thou turnest man to destruction; and sayest, Return, ye children of men.
¶ Likewise, thou son of man, set thy face against the daughters of thy people, which prophesy out of their own heart; and prophesy thou against them,

And in a day when the children of men shall esteem my words as naught and take many of them from the book which thou shalt write, behold, I will raise up another like unto thee; and they shall be had again among the children of men—among as many as shall believe.

Also, thou son of man, shall it not be in the day when I take from them their strength, the joy of their glory, the desire of their eyes, and that whereupon they set their minds, their sons and their daughters,
Thy children shall make haste against thy destroyers; and they that made thee waste shall go forth of thee.
Thy children shall make haste; thy destroyers and they that made thee waste shall go forth of thee.
For a thousand years in thy sight are but as yesterday when it is past, and as a watch in the night.
And the vision of the evening and the morning which was told is true: wherefore shut thou up the vision; for it shall be for many days.
Son of man, behold, they of the house of Israel say, The vision that he seeth is for many days to come, and he prophesieth of the times that are far off.
Even so it shall be in that day when they shall see all these things, then shall they know that the hour is nigh.
Thou carriest them away as with a flood; they are as a sleep: in the morning they are like grass which groweth up.

Yet thou in thy manifold mercies forsookest them not in the wilderness: the pillar of the cloud departed not from them by day, to lead them in the way; neither the pillar of fire by night, to shew them light, and the way wherein they should go.

Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thine inheritance, in the place, O Lord, which thou hast made for thee to dwell in, in the Sanctuary, O Lord, which thy hands have established.
Moreover thou leddest them in the day by a cloudy pillar; and in the night by a pillar of fire, to give them light in the way wherein they should go.
Thou hast planted them, yea, they have taken root: they grow, yea, they bring forth fruit: thou art near in their mouth, and far from their reins.
Therefore the abundance they have gotten, and that which they have laid up, shall they carry away to the brook of the willows.
In the morning it flourisheth, and groweth up; in the evening it is cut down, and withereth.
Thou carriest them away as with a flood; they are as a sleep: in the morning they are like grass which groweth up.
In the day shalt thou make thy plant to grow, and in the morning shalt thou make thy seed to flourish: but the harvest shall be a heap in the day of grief and of desperate sorrow.
¶ Woe unto them that rise up early in the morning, that they may follow strong drink; that continue until night, till wine inflame them!
And he shall be as the light of the morning, when the sun riseth, even a morning without clouds; as the tender grass springing out of the earth by clear shining after rain.
Wo unto them that rise up early in the morning, that they may follow strong drink, that continue until night, and wine inflame them!
For we are consumed by thine anger, and by thy wrath are we troubled.
Who knoweth the power of thine anger? even according to thy fear, so is thy wrath.
Let thine anger be kindled against our enemies; and, in the fury of thine heart, with thy sword avenge us of our wrongs.
Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.
Wilt thou be angry with us for ever? wilt thou draw out thine anger to all generations?

In our daily pursuits in life, of whatever nature and kind, Latter-day
Saints, and especially those who hold important positions in the
Kingdom of God, should maintain a uniform and even temper, both
when at home and when abroad. They should not suffer reverses and
unpleasant circumstances to sour their natures and render them fretful
and unsocial at home, speaking words full of bitterness and…
Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, our secret sins in the light of thy countenance.
Thou hast commanded us to keep thy precepts diligently.
Beware that thou forget not the Lord thy God, in not keeping his commandments, and his judgments, and his statutes, which I command thee this day:
O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.
If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
For all our days are passed away in thy wrath: we spend our years as a tale that is told.
O Lord, I have heard thy speech, and was afraid: O Lord, revive thy work in the midst of the years, in the midst of the years make known; in wrath remember mercy.
I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years are throughout all generations.
Are thy days as the days of man? are thy years as man’s days,
And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed,
The days of our years are threescore years and ten; and if by reason of strength they be fourscore years, yet is their strength labour and sorrow; for it is soon cut off, and we fly away.
And from the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, there shall be a thousand two hundred and ninety days.
For all our days are passed away in thy wrath: we spend our years as a tale that is told.
And thus did pass away the ninety and sixth year; and also the ninety and seventh year; and also the ninety and eighth year; and also the ninety and ninth year;
Who knoweth the power of thine anger? even according to thy fear, so is thy wrath.
For we are consumed by thine anger, and by thy wrath are we troubled.
Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?

Well, let us think of these things, and not get angry. I know that I am a stronger man when the Spirit of God is resting upon me than I am at ordinary times; and I know, when I get angry, that it makes me weak—it takes away my strength. This is the way you feel; for that Spirit makes you mighty and powerful, and fear leaves you. Fear has torment, and torment makes a person weak, and vexes him,…
So teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.
I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.

We are so organized that we can learn but little at a time, and the little we do learn should be that kind of knowledge which will bring to us as individuals and as a community, temporal and eternal salvation. If men were to live until the number of their days should be one hundred years, they still would be but children in the knowledge of this life, and
O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.
For behold, to one is given by the Spirit of God, that he may teach the word of wisdom;
Return, O Lord, how long? and let it repent thee concerning thy servants.
And it shall be given thee from the time thou shalt go, until the time thou shalt return, what thou shalt do.
Repent; or else I will come unto thee quickly, and will fight against them with the sword of my mouth.
And if he trespass against thee the second time, and repent not, nevertheless thou shalt forgive him.
Return unto thy rest, O my soul; for the Lord hath dealt bountifully with thee.
And now thou beholdest, O Lord, that thy servants have done according to thy commandment.
O satisfy us early with thy mercy; that we may rejoice and be glad all our days.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.
O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever.
But rejoice, inasmuch as ye are partakers of Christ’s sufferings; that, when his glory shall be revealed, ye may be glad also with exceeding joy.
Make us glad according to the days wherein thou hast afflicted us, and the years wherein we have seen evil.
Turn thou us unto thee, O Lord, and we shall be turned; renew our days as of old.
and we should rejoice in them; they are the best days we ever saw; and in the midst of the sorrows and afflictions of this life, its trials and temp -
Be patient in afflictions, for thou shalt have many; but endure them, for, lo, I am with thee, even unto the end of thy days.
O satisfy us early with thy mercy; that we may rejoice and be glad all our days.
Let thy work appear unto thy servants, and thy glory unto their children.
Make thy face to shine upon thy servant; and teach me thy statutes.

And by your hands I will work a marvelous work among the children of men, unto the convincing of many of their sins, that they may come unto repentance, and that they may come unto the kingdom of my Father.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, that when I give a commandment to any of the sons of men to do a work unto my name, and those sons of men go with all their might and with all they have to perform that work, and cease not their diligence, and their enemies come upon them and hinder them from performing that work, behold, it behooveth me to require that work no more at the hands of those sons of…
I will declare thy righteousness, and thy works; for they shall not profit thee.
And let the beauty of the Lord our God be upon us: and establish thou the work of our hands upon us; yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.
Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.
For they all made us afraid, saying, Their hands shall be weakened from the work, that it be not done. Now therefore, O God, strengthen my hands.
And now, behold, we are in thine hand: as it seemeth good and right unto thee to do unto us, do.
For thou, Lord, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.
